2605.04196.title: The Impact of Vocabulary Overlaps on Knowledge Transfer in Multilingual Machine Translation
2605.04196.paper_summary: Knowledge transfer, especially across related languages, has been found beneficial for multilingual neural machine translation (MNMT), but some aspects are still under-explored and deserve further investigation. A joint vocabulary is most often applied to form a uniform word embedding space, but since the impact of a disjoint vocabulary on model performance is far less studied, there is no consensus on how much knowledge transfer is mainly due to vocabulary overlap. In this paper, we present systematic experiments with joint and disjoint vocabularies, and auxiliary languages related and unrelated to the source language. We design this experiment in an out-of-domain setup in order to emphasize transfer and the impact of the auxiliary language. As expected, we yield better results with more extensive vocabulary overlaps typical for related languages, but our experiments also show that domain-match and language relatedness are more important than a joint vocabulary.
2605.04196.paper_summary_zh: 知識轉移，特別是在相關語言之間，已被發現對多語言神經機器翻譯（MNMT）有益，但某些方面仍然未被充分探討，值得進一步研究。聯合詞彙通常被應用於形成統一的詞嵌入空間，但由於不相交詞彙對模型性能的影響研究得較少，對於知識轉移主要由詞彙重疊引起的程度尚無共識。在本文中，我們提出了針對聯合和不相交詞彙，以及與源語言相關和無關的輔助語言進行的系統實驗。我們在一個非領域設置中設計了這個實驗，以強調轉移和輔助語言的影響。正如預期，我們在相關語言中更廣泛的詞彙重疊下獲得了更好的結果，但我們的實驗也顯示，領域匹配和語言相關性比聯合詞彙更為重要。
2605.04196.author: Oona Itkonen et.al.
